verse_specific_anchors: the three-times structure (past / present / future): 'never was a time / nor will be' → 'predates the founders / outlasts the language'
the plural of selves (I, you, these kings, all of us) → 'this team, future teams, prior teams as transient custodians of one durable need'
ataḥ param ('hereafter / beyond this point') → 'will not pack it away when they leave'
explicit non-commitment to Advaita/Vishishtadvaita ontology — preserved as non-commitment in the engineering analog
verdict_reason: Tested against three random verses from Chapters 6, 9, and 17: BG 6.5 (uddhared ātmanātmānaṁ — lift the self by the self), BG 9.27 (yat karoṣi yad aśnāsi — whatever you do, eat, offer), BG 17.20 (dātavyam iti yad dānaṁ — gift given without expecting return). The 2.12 engineering analog (the requirement that predates the founders and outlasts the language) cannot equally apply to any of these. The custodianship-under-team-turnover anchor is uniquely a 2.12 reading — it relies on the past-future-eternality structure of the verse, which the test verses lack. PASS.
